item.title: Trans Girl Tuesday - Transition Selfie 2.5 years
item.description: I started HRT at 32 years old, and given I had a good photo and it's a good milestone and today's tuesday, I figured I'd post it here! I still have a lot of confidence issues, but seeing these photos next to eachother it's also pretty clear to see how far I've come. Definitely super happy to see that. Please excuse the scraggly hair, it's slightly damp :D
item.dc:creator: FoxyEllie

item.title: Little video Dump for your Wednesday!
item.description:
Kristin Key
I can tell you, it's equally fun when my pigs have to get their hooves done and my male also has to get his tusks trimmed
Yea yea... banana cleaner
We had a family come meet the foster pups today, this may be a bit of my own story
I want turkeys
Horses=big, crazy dogs
Me when grocery shopping and people insist on basically breathing down my neck for no reason.
This is what it sounds like when doves try
Good job sir
Going someone might see this and be in that area and go save him.
I've got fosters from Pinal County Animal Care & Control who are currently fighting to contain a distemper outbreak. This is in central Arizona. If you have any room to foster puppies, people keep just dropping them off despite pleas to not do so since puppies are so susceptible to distemper. They need fosters or adopters. Adoptions are free thanks to Bissell from May 1st-15th. See my two other recent posts for my fosters. The gray faced one, we call Braveheart, may have met his forever family today. Please Imgur, do your thing, down vote whatever you like, just need some people to help the puppies and kitties. I know you can work magic, I've seen it.
item.dc:creator: Herperchick

item.title: In 1993 my dad called into a radio station and won tickets to a concert. My mom was the dj doing the on air giveaway. That’s how they met and today they’ve been married 31 years.

item.title: Good News-letter #4 - April edition!
item.description:
Hello guys, gals, and non-binary pals! Here’s a fresh batch of good news for you to digest!
Japan uses money tied up in Western assets to back a loan for Ukraine - https://thesarkariform.com/japan-just-gave-ukraine-3-billion-from-russian-assets/
Unauthorized mob of men who forcibly removed woman from a town hall receive laundry list of charges - https://www.ktvb.com/article/news/local/idaho/six-charged-coeur-dalene-town-hall-incident/277-204bf517-c188-401f-a904-7859511da073
Pentagon to resume gender-affirming care for service members - https://www.ktvb.com/article/news/local/idaho/six-charged-coeur-dalene-town-hall-incident/277-204bf517-c188-401f-a904-7859511da073
Paris has cut its air pollution by half - https://www.reddit.com/r/goodnews/s/WAgtKI8RAC
Planet exceeds 40% clean power threshold - https://electrek.co/2025/04/07/world-surges-past-40-percent-clean-power-in-record-renewable-boom/
Iconic “gayborhood” builds affordable housing for elderly members of LGBTQIA+ community - https://www.goodgoodgood.co/articles/oak-lawn-place-affordable-housing-lgbtq-texas
That’s all for now my friends! If this helped you today, consider sharing with others. I love you and there’s nothing you can do about it! See you at the end of May! Want to be notified on the next one? Comment “Count me in!” Missed last month? Here ya go - https://imgur.com/gallery/7GOl33U
item.dc:creator: rabbihimself

item.title: What Happened Today #89
item.description: ?? Trump’s default playbook today is clear: fortify the border with troop-policed “defense strips,” mass-deport declaring Haitian migrants as terrorists, and even using postage images as clues to their whereabouts. Judges, watchdogs, and charities are pushing back, but the squeeze keeps tightening until enough of us raise hell. There are marches and events all over for May Day--tomorrow. Go to Mobilize.us to learn how you can bring about change in your community. Don’t rely on an algorithm. Get this in your inbox every night, free and private, so you never miss what’s really going on. Add your email at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/military-border 2. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-could-get-abregogarcia 3. https://go.govbrief.today/invasion-judicial-review 4. https://go.govbrief.today/cbp-probablecause 5. https://go.govbrief.today/haitian-terrorists 6. https://go.govbrief.today/postal-data-immigration 7. https://go.govbrief.today/attorneys-children 8. https://go.govbrief.today/dugan-suspended 9. https://go.govbrief.today/amazon-trump-tariff 10. https://go.govbrief.today/auto-tariffs-eased 11. https://go.govbrief.today/cfpb-firing-halted 12. https://go.govbrief.today/cps-schools-discrimination 13. https://go.govbrief.today/harvard-internal-report 14. https://go.govbrief.today/holocaust-board-fired 15. https://go.govbrief.today/radio-europe-funds 16. https://go.govbrief.today/cpb-sues-trump 17. https://go.govbrief.today/yemen-dozens-dead 18. https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-probe-blocked 19. https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-wps-trump 20. https://go.govbrief.today/rfk-do-research 21. https://go.govbrief.today/fl-house-flouride 22. https://go.govbrief.today/ed-martin-answers 23. https://go.govbrief.today/yle-healthwebinar

item.title: Bruce Willis signed his $5 million contract for "Die Hard" in November 1987. The amount stunned Hollywood. At the time, only a handful of A-list actors Dustin Hoffman, Warren Beatty, and Robert Redford had ever commanded such a salary.
item.description: Bruce Willis signed his $5 million contract for "Die Hard" in November 1987. The amount stunned Hollywood. At the time, only a handful of A-list actors Dustin Hoffman, Warren Beatty, and Robert Redford had ever commanded such a salary. Willis had no box office draw, no prior action experience, and no hit films behind him. What he had was a semi-successful TV show "Moonlighting" and a reputation as a comedic actor with a wisecracking style. When 20th Century Fox announced Willis as the lead in their $28 million action thriller, the backlash from both media and industry insiders was immediate and intense. He was filming "Moonlighting" on a grueling schedule when the deal closed. The show aired weekly, and the stress of bouncing between that set and the "Die Hard" production only intensified speculation that he was in over his head. Action stars of that era like Sylvester Stallone and Arnold Schwarzenegger were sculpted, stoic, and projected unshakable strength. Willis had a receding hairline, a lean frame, and a smirking charm. The studio didn’t even put him on the initial poster; early teaser posters of "Die Hard" showed only the Nakatomi Plaza building engulfed in explosions. His name appeared in small font. Executives feared that using his image might hurt ticket sales. Inside the studio, his casting had sparked months of internal tension. Richard Gere, Burt Reynolds, Mel Gibson, and even Frank Sinatra due to a contractual obligation from the 1960s had been considered for the role of John McClane. Director John McTiernan was hesitant about Willis, skeptical of whether he could sell the emotional beats while delivering convincing physicality in a film full of explosions, gunfire, and death-defying stunts. But producer Joel Silver saw potential. He believed that audiences would connect with a hero who looked vulnerable and scared rather than invincible and armored. That vulnerability was the very essence of John McClane a man caught in the wrong place at the wrong time, with nothing but desperation and grit to survive. Willis approached the role without any attempt to mimic the established action formula. He kept McClane grounded afraid, bleeding, sarcastic, and improvising his way through terror. The now-iconic moment where McClane walks barefoot across broken glass wasn’t in the original script. It came from an on-set discussion between McTiernan and Willis about how to physically show a man breaking down without losing his resolve. Willis threw himself into every stunt the insurance policy allowed, often showing up on set even when "Moonlighting" night shoots had kept him working into the early morning. That fatigue added an authentic weariness to his performance. Critics were skeptical right up to the premiere. Some trade magazines published snide headlines mocking the $5 million paycheck. One gossip column joked that the only explosions in "Die Hard" would be in Fox’s accounting department. But when the film opened on July 15, 1988, it took in $140,000 per screen in its first weekend. The numbers kept climbing. Audiences embraced Willis's everyman quality. Unlike the musclebound titans of the genre, he sweated, cursed, limped, and joked his way through gunfights and broken elevators. By August, "Die Hard" was a full-blown hit, earning over $140 million worldwide and redefining the modern action film. The internal gamble had paid off, and Willis’s image on later posters was enlarged and spotlighted. That $5 million bet changed the perception of who could be an action star. Studios began seeking leading men who didn’t fit the mold flawed, witty, reluctant heroes in impossible situations. The risk 20th Century Fox took in trusting an unproven actor with a genre-defining role paved the way for a new generation of action storytelling, forever altering the studio's approach to casting and character
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: Yes, But
item.dc:creator: thequiz

item.title: I'm at a loss
item.description: I had a lung biopsy yesterday and i was scared my lung might collapse (it happens, but rarely so) and that i would be awake during the procedure. It went off without a hitch. Unfortunately, it turns out the pet scan was wrong, it was cancer. This means my stage 3 diagnosis has been upgraded to stage 4! Yay upgrades! The good news is they have a plan. Aggressive chemo. I think. I'm waiting on a call from my primary oncologist to confirm, but thats what she said would be the plan. I was diagnosed with stage 3 colon cancer last year. It was so bad they had to operate immediately. Went from 1st routine colonoscopy to cancer removal in 8 days. Then 6 months of radiation and chemo. Now this. I'm just yelling into the void here so if you got this far, thank you for listening. I hope you have a peaceful day.
item.dc:creator: SkamanSam

item.title: Who wants to get a head of lettuce ready?
item.dc:creator: ubermonkey21

item.title: A local millionaire responsible for a 2nd death is avoiding charges.
item.description:
Jeff Knight is the owner of the boat that hit the ferry. He's a bigwig millionaire that owns Janus Live(A music venue where I live) in St Petersburg Florida. He has 3 previous DUI's and was given probation in 2012 when a passenger on his boat fell overboard and died. On Tuesday he was driving his boat and hit a ferry killing one and injuring 10 people. He then left the scene of the accident. So far the city is refusing to arrest him or file any charges. Locals are already starting to boycott the venue over this, I really hope something comes of it and this guy doesn't get to keep ruining lives.
This is the man who lost his life to Jeff Knights gross negligence.
The police are saying he took a breathalyzer later and blew 0.0 but that's BS this guy is well known for being a coke head and alcoholic and getting away with things constantly by local police. It's insane how much pull this guy has because he owns local businesses and a music venue in the city.
Venue: https://maps.app.goo.gl/NheriNka2EcezTJh8 Full story: https://baynews9.com/fl/tampa/news/2025/04/29/ferry-crash-investigation-congoing--no-charges-filed-as-of-yet?cid=id-app15_m-share_s-web_cmp-app_launch_august2020_c-producer_posts_po-organic&sfnsn=mo
item.dc:creator: Sensiblyinteresting

item.title: Saudi Arabia has deployed solar-powered laser beacons in the Nafud Desert to guide lost travellers to water sources.
item.description: These beacons emit visible laser signals at night, making it easier for people in distress to find their way to safety, especially in the vast and often barren desert landscape.
item.dc:creator: LunaAtlas

item.dc:creator: countervail247365

item.title: Good news!
item.description:
Update from: https://imgur.com/gallery/yxOEg4Q The Union served our GM with notice of intent. Our GM shared it with the Board, Legal, etc. and...
We have a new GM and two new Board members and they inherited a mess. They may not be pleased about staff’s position, but they won't contest it...which means we won't have to hold a formal election and delay negotiations for the next contract.
My immediate supervisor was on vacation when it all happened. When she learned my department had 100% support for unionization she took it as a personal grievance against her. If I had made a list of 100 reasons for/against, I guarantee my boss's feelings wouldn't have been on it. We're officially entering the "find out phase" after years of bad management practices.
Misu tax. Hope is a dangerous thing, friend. Feed it & wield it for good. It *might* just make a difference. Please be a thoughtful human & enjoy your day!
item.dc:creator: oneduringwanderer

item.title: The kittens found a hidey hole!
item.description: Willow was suspiciously the only kitten in view when I came in, but big sister Meadow’s face peeked out and I found them! I put those blankets over there to cover the electrical cords but if they like to snuggle there, more power to them! Get it? Power?? I’ll see myself out
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

Pinup Tax “Life should not be a journey to the grave with the intention of arriving safely in a pretty and well preserved body, but rather to skid in broadside in a cloud of smoke, thoroughly used up, totally worn out, and loudly proclaiming "Wow! What a Ride!” ― Hunter S. Thompson
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator
